info.title: tickward API
info.summary: Versioned API for tickward projects, timers, spaces, and public share links.
info.description: The tickward API lets users automate countdown projects, timers, spaces, and public share links. Use Bearer API keys created in tickward settings. Read-only keys can inspect data. Full-access keys can create, update, and delete resources. Agents should prefer read-only keys for answering questions, ask for explicit confirmation before destructive changes, and use ISO 8601 datetimes plus IANA timezones when creating timers.

tags.name: Timers
tags.description: Timers track countdowns, notify state, recurrence, and sharing metadata.

tags.name: Spaces
tags.description: Spaces organize timers inside a project.

get.description: List projects owned by the API key user. Use this first when an agent needs to resolve which project the user means. Supports cursor pagination with `after`, `before`, and `limit`.

metadata.description: List projects owned by the API key user. Use this first when an agent needs to resolve which project the user means. Supports cursor pagination with `after`, `before`, and `limit`.

post.description: Create a project owned by the API key user. The body can include spaces and timers for one atomic project setup. Agents should call POST /projects/preview first for multi-resource creates, then send expected_plan_hash with the create request.

metadata.description: Create a project owned by the API key user. The body can include spaces and timers for one atomic project setup. Agents should call POST /projects/preview first for multi-resource creates, then send expected_plan_hash with the create request.

get.description: Retrieve project metadata and counts. Use timers and spaces endpoints to inspect project contents.

metadata.description: Retrieve project metadata and counts. Use timers and spaces endpoints to inspect project contents.

patch.description: Rename or recolor a project. Requires a full-access key.

metadata.description: Rename or recolor a project. Requires a full-access key.

delete.description: Delete a project and its related timers, spaces, share links, access tokens, and notification records. Use `dry_run=true` first to preview the cascade without mutating data. Agents must ask for explicit user confirmation before applying the delete.

metadata.description: Delete a project and its related timers, spaces, share links, access tokens, and notification records. Use `dry_run=true` first to preview the cascade without mutating data. Agents must ask for explicit user confirmation before applying the delete.

get.description: List all timers in a project. Use this to answer questions about deadlines, archived timers, notification state, and shared timers.

metadata.description: List all timers in a project. Use this to answer questions about deadlines, archived timers, notification state, and shared timers.

post.description: Create a timer in a project. Requires a full-access key. Provide a clear `label`, an ISO 8601 `target_date`, and an IANA `timezone`; create the space first before sending `space_id`.

metadata.description: Create a timer in a project. Requires a full-access key. Provide a clear `label`, an ISO 8601 `target_date`, and an IANA `timezone`; create the space first before sending `space_id`.
Retrieve one timer by id.

patch.description: Update a timer, including label, target date, timezone, archived state, notification settings, recurrence, pinning, image, or space assignment. Requires a full-access key.

metadata.description: Update a timer, including label, target date, timezone, archived state, notification settings, recurrence, pinning, image, or space assignment. Requires a full-access key.

delete.description: Delete a timer and related share and notification records. Requires a full-access key. Agents must ask for explicit user confirmation before calling this endpoint.

metadata.description: Delete a timer and related share and notification records. Requires a full-access key. Agents must ask for explicit user confirmation before calling this endpoint.

get.description: List spaces in a project. Spaces are lightweight categories for organizing timers.

metadata.description: List spaces in a project. Spaces are lightweight categories for organizing timers.

post.description: Create a space in a project. Requires a full-access key. Create spaces before assigning timers to them.

metadata.description: Create a space in a project. Requires a full-access key. Create spaces before assigning timers to them.
Retrieve one space by id.

patch.description: Rename or recolor a space. Requires a full-access key.

metadata.description: Rename or recolor a space. Requires a full-access key.

delete.description: Delete a space and unassign its timers from that space. Use `dry_run=true` first to preview affected timers without mutating data. Timers are not deleted.

metadata.description: Delete a space and unassign its timers from that space. Use `dry_run=true` first to preview affected timers without mutating data. Timers are not deleted.

get.description: Returns the public share links that have been created for timers in this project.

metadata.description: Returns the public share links that have been created for timers in this project.

post.summary: Create or reuse a shared timer link
post.description: Create or return the stable public link for a timer. Requires a full-access key. This operation is idempotent for the same user and timer.

metadata.description: Create or return the stable public link for a timer. Requires a full-access key. This operation is idempotent for the same user and timer.

get.description: Retrieve one public timer link by share id.

metadata.description: Retrieve one public timer link by share id.

delete.description: Delete a public timer link and clear the timer shared state. Requires a full-access key.

metadata.description: Delete a public timer link and clear the timer shared state. Requires a full-access key.

get.description: Return machine-readable API features and limits. This endpoint does not require an API key and is safe for agents and self-hosted deployments to call before choosing a workflow.

metadata.description: Return machine-readable API features and limits. This endpoint does not require an API key and is safe for agents and self-hosted deployments to call before choosing a workflow.

post.description: Validate a project creation payload and return the projects, spaces, and timers that would be created. This endpoint does not mutate data and does not record idempotency.

metadata.description: Validate a project creation payload and return the projects, spaces, and timers that would be created. This endpoint does not mutate data and does not record idempotency.

bearerAuth.description: Use a tickward API key from Settings. API keys start with `tw_`. Store keys as secrets and send them as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

After.name: after
After.description: Return projects after this project id. Cannot be combined with `before`.

Before.name: before
Before.description: Return projects before this project id. Cannot be combined with `after`.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Maximum number of projects to return.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional for POST, PATCH, and DELETE requests. Generate a random UUID with an operation prefix, for example timer-create-${crypto.randomUUID()}. Reuse the same key with the same method, path, query, and JSON body to safely retry one logical write for up to 24 hours. Reusing it with a different request returns 409. The server stores only a hash of the key.

DryRun.name: dry_run
DryRun.description: When true, return a preview of what would change without mutating data or recording idempotency.

RateLimitLimit.description: Maximum requests available in the current window.

RateLimitRemaining.description: Requests remaining in the current window.

RateLimitReset.description: Seconds until the current rate-limit window resets.

IdempotencyReplayed.description: Present with value true when this response was replayed from an earlier idempotent request.

IdempotencyKeyExpiresAt.description: When the idempotency replay record expires.

RequestId.description: Unique id for this HTTP attempt.

CorrelationId.description: Correlation id for grouping related calls.

UnauthorizedError.description: Missing bearer credential, insufficient scope, or read-only credential used for a write request.

ValidationError.description: Invalid request body or query parameters.

NotFoundError.description: Resource not found or not owned by the API key user.

IdempotencyConflictError.description: The Idempotency-Key is still processing or was already used for a different method, path, query, or JSON body.

error.message: Idempotency-Key was already used for a different request.
A request with this Idempotency-Key is still processing.

ProjectCreateResponse.description: Created project with created spaces and timers.

ConflictError.description: Conflict, including idempotency conflicts or plan hash mismatch.

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. Prefer this over message text for branching logic.

request_id.description: Request id for this HTTP attempt. Also returned in the Request-Id header.

correlation_id.description: Correlation id for grouping related calls. Also returned in the Correlation-Id header.

retryable.description: Whether retrying the same request can be useful.

hint.description: Short instruction for humans or agents on how to resolve the error.

errors.description: Field-level errors using JSON Pointer paths when applicable.

error.message: We found an error with one or more fields in the request.

remediation.hint: Fix the fields listed in errors and retry the request.

errors.remediation: Fix the fields listed in details or errors and retry the request.

ListProject.description: Paginated list response. When `has_more` is true, call the same endpoint with `after` set to the last item id.

ListTimer.description: List response for timers in a project.

ListSpace.description: List response for spaces in a project.

ListShare.description: List response for public timer links in a project.

expected_plan_hash.description: Optional plan hash returned by POST /projects/preview. When present, the create request fails if the current body no longer matches the preview.

spaces.description: Spaces to create with the project. Timers can be nested inside a space.

timers.description: Timers to create with the project. Use space_id only when referencing a space id supplied in spaces[].id.

example.label: Flight to Tokyo
example.description: Pack before leaving for the airport.

ColorInput.description: Use a hex color such as `#2563eb`. Empty string clears color where supported.

IsoDateTime.description: ISO 8601 datetime string. Prefer UTC with `Z` or an explicit offset.

Timezone.description: IANA timezone name, for example `Europe/Warsaw` or `America/New_York`.

type.description: Resource type affected by the operation.

NestedProjectTimerCreateRequest.description: Timer payload nested inside a project space. Do not set space_id; the containing space is used.

path.description: JSON Pointer path in the preview request body.
